english: to decrease / to diminish / to ease up / to slacken / to let up
notes: Separable verb (nach- + lassen). Used both intransitively (pain, wind, interest decreases) and transitively (to reduce a price, to slacken a rope). The intransitive meaning is by far the most common.

sentences.english: The rain is slowly letting up.

sentences.german: Die Schmerzen haben nach der Behandlung nachgelassen.
sentences.english: The pain has eased after the treatment.

sentences.german: Sein Interesse an dem Projekt lässt immer mehr nach.
sentences.english: His interest in the project is decreasing more and more.
